heading: Special postage stamps
label: 414 Special postage stamps
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (e), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 105–41Aug. 13, 1997

Amendments2019—Subsec. (h). substituted “2027” for “2019”.Pub. L. 116–922015—Subsec. (c)(1). , inserted at end of concluding provisions “An agency that receives amounts from the Postal Service under this paragraph shall use the amounts for breast cancer research.”Pub. Reporting Requirements, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–150, § 2121 Stat. 1820Dec. 21, 2007“The National Institutes of Health and the Department of Defense shall each submit to Congress and the Government Accountability Office an annual report concerning the use of any amounts that it received under , United States Code, including a description of any significant advances or accomplishments, during the year covered by the report, that were funded, in whole or in part, with such amounts.”section 414(c) of title 39

Report by Comptroller General of United States, , , provided that:Pub. L. 106–253, § 3(b)114 Stat. 637July 28, 2000“No later than 3 months and no earlier than 6 months before the date as of which , United States Code (as amended by this section) is scheduled to expire, the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to the Congress a report on the operation of such section. Such report shall be in addition to the report required by [set out below], and shall address at least the same matters as were required to be included in that earlier report.”section 414 of title 39section 2(b) of Public Law 105–41, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–41, § 2(b)111 Stat. 1120Aug. 13, 1997“No later than 3 months (but no earlier than 6 months) before the end of the 2-year period referred to in , United States Code (as amended by subsection (a)), the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to the Congress a report on the operation of such section. Such report shall include—section 414(g) of title 39“(1)an evaluation of the effectiveness and the appropriateness of the authority provided by such section as a means of fund-raising; and“(2)a description of the monetary and other resources required of the Postal Service in carrying out such section.”

L. 105–41, § 2(b)111 Stat. 1120Aug. 13, 1997“No later than 3 months (but no earlier than 6 months) before the end of the 2-year period referred to in , United States Code (as amended by subsection (a)), the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to the Congress a report on the operation of such section. children.label: a
children.text: In order to afford the public a convenient way to contribute to funding for breast cancer research, the Postal Service shall establish a special rate of postage for first-class mail under this section.
shall be equal to the regular first-class rate of postage, plus a differential of not less than 15 percent;
shall be set by the Governors in accordance with such procedures as the Governors shall by regulation prescribe (in lieu of the procedures under chapter 36); and
shall be offered as an alternative to the regular first-class rate of postage.

children.label: A
children.text: 70 percent to the National Institutes of Health; and

children.label: B
children.text: the remainder to the Department of Defense.

children.label: A
children.text: the total amounts received by the Postal Service that it would not have received but for the enactment of this section, reduced by

children.label: B
children.text: an amount sufficient to cover reasonable costs incurred by the Postal Service in carrying out this section, including those attributable to the printing, sale, and distribution of stamps under this section,

directly or indirectly cause a net decrease in total funds received by the National Institutes of Health, the Department of Defense, or any other agency of the Government (or any component or program thereof) below the level that would otherwise have been received but for the enactment of this section; or
affect regular first-class rates of postage or any other regular rates of postage.

children.label: e
children.text: Special postage stamps under this section shall be made available to the public beginning on such date as the Postal Service shall by regulation prescribe, but in no event later than 12 months after the date of the enactment of this section.
the total amount described in subsection (c)(2)(A) which was received by the Postal Service during the period covered by such report; and
of the amount under paragraph (1), how much (in the aggregate and by category) was required for the purposes described in subsection (c)(2)(B).

children.label: g
children.text: For purposes of section 416 (including any regulation prescribed under subsection (e)(1)(C) of that section), the special postage stamp issued under this section shall not apply to any limitation relating to whether more than 1 semipostal may be offered for sale at the same time.

children.label: h
children.text: This section shall cease to be effective after .December 31, 2027
